intelligence.hypothesis: Insufficient telemetry; awaiting next valid capture.

vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The registry is largely non-representative of the current visual data. P1 (String of Nickels) is absent. P2 (Mexican Mint) and P4 (Silver Guest) are not identifiable as distinct specimens. Only P3 (Pothos) is present, anchored by the white rabbit figurine.

inventory_reconciliation.P1: Systemic Loss: No plant matter detected in the yellow pot.
inventory_reconciliation.P2: Systemic Loss: No identifiable mint foliage present.
inventory_reconciliation.P3: Present: Pothos specimen remains, though growth is minimal.
inventory_reconciliation.P4: Systemic Loss: No identifiable silver guest foliage present.

plant_audit.P1_Yellow_Pot: Empty soil surface; no signs of life.
plant_audit.P2_Black_Pot: Appears to be used as a vessel for non-botanical items or is currently fallow.
plant_audit.P3_Pothos_Pot: Pothos remains the primary occupant; white rabbit anchor is stable.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Soil appears dark and potentially over-saturated in all pots.
biome_observations.surface_debris: Introduction of a metal spoon in the yellow pot (T-1) and a white plastic/ceramic object (possibly a sensor or tool) in the yellow pot (CURRENT).

temporal_deltas.T_minus_2_to_T_minus_1: Introduction of a metal spoon into the yellow pot.
temporal_deltas.T_minus_1_to_CURRENT: Removal of the spoon; introduction of a white, L-shaped structural object into the yellow pot.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is in a state of severe neglect or experimental transition. The lack of visible foliage for P1, P2, and P4 suggests these specimens have perished or were removed. P3 is the only surviving occupant, though its health cannot be confirmed as thriving due to low light and lack of visible new growth.
anomalies: Absence of expected flora in P1, P2, and P4.
vision_report.narrative_description: The botanical environment has transitioned from a multi-plant setup to a largely fallow state. The yellow pot, originally designated for P1, is now being used for non-botanical interventions (spoon, sensor). The Pothos in P3 is the sole survivor. The environment appears to be shifting from a botanical display to a technical or observational testbed.
